question_text: A coal-fired power station generates 500 MW of electricity. The power station burns 1500 tonnes of coal per day. Coal contains 25 MJ of energy per kilogram. Calculate the efficiency of the power station. (Give your answer to 2 significant figures.)
explanation: First convert mass to kg: 1500 tonnes = 1,500,000 kg. Total energy input = 1,500,000 × 25 = 37,500 MJ = 37.5 GJ per day. Convert to joules: 37.5 × 10⁹ J. Energy output = 500 × 10⁶ W × 86,400 s = 4.32 × 10¹⁶ J per day. Efficiency = (4.32 × 10¹⁶) / (3.75 × 10¹⁶) × 100 = 33%.
